properties.name: Knowledge Graph
properties.claim: An interlinked set of facts describing entities and their relationships in a human- and machine-understandable form.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Jesús Barrasa
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Jim Webber
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: O'Reilly Media / Neo4j
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Organizing Principle
properties.claim: A knowledge graph needs an organizing principle that supplies context and acts as a contract between graph data and its human or software consumers.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Knowledge Graph
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.note: organizing principle structures the knowledge graph

properties.name: Labeled Property Graph
properties.claim: Labeled property graphs provide labeled nodes, typed directed relationships, and properties as a practical base.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Organizing Principle
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.note: property graph realizes the organizing principle

properties.name: Just-Enough Semantics
properties.claim: Semantic richness should be driven by intended use; avoid building an overly ambitious metamodel before the use case requires it.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Organizing Principle
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.note: semantics grow incrementally within the organizing principle

properties.name: Stable Identity
properties.claim: Duplicate prevention requires stable identity, constraints, and careful decomposition of node and relationship upserts.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Organizing Principle
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.note: identity is part of the contract

properties.name: Explicit Relationship Types
properties.claim: Explicit labels and relationship types improve interpretability and query planning.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Stable Identity
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Metadata Knowledge Graph
properties.claim: A metadata graph maps tasks, datasets, platforms, consumers, owners, lineage and impact; relationships connect data and metadata so consumers can answer a domain question and also understand source and governance provenance.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Provenance
properties.claim: Provenance links let a graph record where each fact came from and how it flows.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Dependency Graph
properties.claim: Dependency graphs support impact propagation, validation, cycle detection, and root-cause analysis.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Provenance
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Things Not Strings
properties.claim: Semantic search improves when unstructured text is connected to stable concepts rather than matched only as ambiguous strings.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Organizing Principle
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Natural Language Graph Interface
properties.claim: Natural-language questions and generated answers can be grounded in the graph's organizing principle.
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide

properties.name: Things Not Strings
properties.source: Building Knowledge Graphs: A Practitioner's Guide
